name: Cerro de San Vicente and Cabeza del Oso Loop
name_original: Cerro de San Vicente – circular por la senda Cabeza del Oso y Pelados (PR-TO 21)
description: Hike the moderate 6.5-mile Cerro de San Vicente and Cabeza del Oso Loop in Toledo, offering picturesque views and varied foliage.
region_intro: Hiking around El Real De San Vicente offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra de San Vicente mountain range. The region features significant elevations such as Cabeza del Oso and Pelados mountains, alongside Cerro de San Vicente, which reaches 1,320 meters. Extensive chestnut, pine, and oak forests characterize the area, complemented by numerous springs and fountains. The Bonal slope adds varied tree foliage to the natural environment. Best hiking trails in El Real De San Vicente The most popular hiking route is Cabeza del Oso and Pelados Trail (PR-TO 21), an 8.8 miles (14.2 km) moderate trail that takes 4 hours 41 minutes to complete. This route traverses picturesque areas, including the scenic Bonal slope. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cerro de San Vicente and Cabeza del Oso Loop, a moderate 6.5 miles (10.5 km) path. This trail offers expansive views from Cerro de San Vicente, standing at 1,320 meters. Local hikers also love the Sierra Cruces – Puerto del Piélago loop from Campamento El Piélago, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 42 minutes. Hiking around El Real De San Vicente is defined by mountain elevations, extensive forests, and numerous water sources. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging treks. The routes in El Real De San Vicente are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore El Real De San Vicente's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in El Real De San Vicente?
A: El Real De San Vicente offers a diverse network of over 30 hiking trails. These range from easy paths suitable for all fitness levels to more demanding treks, ensuring options for every hiker.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hikes in El Real De San Vicente?
A: Yes, there are 5 easy routes in the area, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed stroll. These paths are generally up to 2 hours long with minimal elevation gain, making them suitable for all fitness levels.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in El Real De San Vicente?
A: The fresh environment, especially in spring, makes for enjoyable hiking conditions. The region's abundant chestnut, pine, and oak forests also offer varied tree foliage, making autumn a picturesque time to visit. While not explicitly mentioned for winter, the diverse landscapes are generally accessible year-round.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in El Real De San Vicente?
A: Hikers in El Real De San Vicente will encounter diverse landscapes, including significant elevations like the Cabeza del Oso and Pelados mountains, and the Cerro de San Vicente (1,320m). The region is characterized by extensive chestnut, pine, and oak forests, the scenic Bonal slope, and numerous springs and fountains.

Q: Are there any circular walks in El Real De San Vicente?
A: Yes, the area features several circular routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018248647/cabeza-del-oso-and-pelados-trail-pr-to-21" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeza del Oso and Pelados Trail (PR-TO 21)&lt;/a&gt;, a 14.2 km moderate circular route. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018243665/cerro-de-san-vicente-and-cabeza-del-oso-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de San Vicente and Cabeza del Oso Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 10.5 km long.

Q: What are some interesting natural features or landmarks to see along the trails?
A: Hikers can discover several natural and historical points of interest. The village itself boasts a 'Sendero de las Fuentes' (Path of the Fountains) with 17 different fountains, including the historic Fuente de los Caños. You can also find a charming wooden bridge over a waterfall within the village. For panoramic views, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente Hill (1320m)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in El Real De San Vicente?
A: The routes in El Real De San Vicente are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning panoramic views from elevated points, and the well-maintained paths through the extensive forests.

Q: Are there any routes that offer expansive views?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018243665/cerro-de-san-vicente-and-cabeza-del-oso-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de San Vicente and Cabeza del Oso Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers expansive views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de San Vicente (1320m)&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to marvel at the Toledo area, including the Sierra de Gredos park and Alto Tiétar.

Q: Are there any trails that feature water sources or waterfalls?
A: Yes, water is a prominent feature in the landscape. The village of El Real de San Vicente has a route known as the 'Sendero de las Fuentes' (Path of the Fountains), which showcases 17 different fountains, including the historic Fuente de los Caños. Additionally, a charming wooden bridge over a waterfall can be found within the village.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes in this region?
A: Hikes in El Real De San Vicente vary in duration. Moderate trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018248647/cabeza-del-oso-and-pelados-trail-pr-to-21" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeza del Oso and Pelados Trail (PR-TO 21)&lt;/a&gt; typically take around 4 hours and 41 minutes. Shorter options, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31466527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Cruces – Puerto del Piélago loop from Campamento El Piélago&lt;/a&gt;, can be completed in about 2 hours and 42 minutes.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical sites near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, near the trails, you can find a convent and the hermitage of Virgen de Piélago, offering cultural points of interest amidst your hikes. The historic Fuente de los Caños, dating back to 1794, is also part of the 'Sendero de las Fuentes' within the village.
tips:
The marked circular route of the Cabeza del Oso and Pelados trail has a total length of 15 kilometers, starting from the village of El Real de San Vicente and crossing one of the most picturesque areas of Toledo.
This section of the trail, just under a kilometer long, runs alongside the Bonal slope and crosses a beautiful landscape where, depending on the time of year, you'll enjoy a varied palette of colors in the tree foliage.
Located at 1,320 meters above sea level, the top of Cerro San Vicente offers wonderful views of this area of Toledo and you can even see the Sierra de Gredos park and Alto Tiétar.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike this loop and what is its difficulty?
faqs.answer: The Cerro de San Vicente and Cabeza del Oso Loop is a moderate hike that typically takes around 3 hours and 38 minutes to complete. It involves an elevation gain of approximately 579 meters, requiring a good level of fitness and sure-footedness.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail officially starts from the village of El Real de San Vicente. While specific parking details aren't provided, starting from the village is the common access point for this well-marked route. It's advisable to check local parking options upon arrival in El Real de San Vicente.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied tree foliage, providing a diverse palette of colors depending on the season. This suggests it can be enjoyed throughout the year, with spring and autumn likely offering the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Cerro de San Vicente and Cabeza del Oso Loop?
faqs.answer: The route features mostly accessible paths with varied tree foliage, offering beautiful views in all directions. While generally accessible, some sections may require sure-footedness due to the moderate difficulty and elevation changes.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: There is no specific restriction mentioned regarding dogs on the Cerro de San Vicente and Cabeza del Oso Loop. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural areas, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for hiking in Sierra de San Vicente?
faqs.answer: The Cerro de San Vicente and Cabeza del Oso Loop (PR-TO 21) is a public trail starting from El Real de San Vicente. There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access this trail or the general Sierra de San Vicente area for hiking.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll traverse the scenic Cabeza del Oso and Pelados Trail. You'll also reach the San Vicente Hill (1320m), offering expansive views of the surrounding Toledo region.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is part of a network of trails. It intersects with and utilizes sections of other paths, including the Senda de Viriato, Cabeza del Oso y Pelados, and the Camino del Vijuelo.

faqs.question: Is the Cerro de San Vicente and Cabeza del Oso Loop suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and approximately 3 hours and 38 minutes duration, this trail is best suited for families with older children who have some hiking experience and a good level of fitness. Younger children might find the length and elevation gain challenging.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this loop?
faqs.answer: For this moderate day hike, it's recommended to bring plenty of water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for varied weather conditions. A map or GPS device is also advisable, though the route is well-marked as PR-TO 21.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restrooms near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from El Real de San Vicente, a village where you can typically find local amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and public restrooms. It's a good idea to stock up on supplies or grab a meal before or after your hike.

waypoints.name: Cabeza del Oso and Pelados Trail
